displayTitle: Does It Stand Out of the Grass?

beats.narration: The calls that earn your stamp are small excursions in a moving background. The question isn't what's on screen, but does it stand out.

payload.title: SIGNAL-TO-NOISE RATIO
payload.sub: Detection is a ratio, not an amplitude.
payload.panelLabel: ET PROBE // FLAW IN A NOISY BACKGROUND

beats.narration: Signal-to-noise ratio is the notch response divided by the noise band, both peak-to-peak, same channel, same settings. Aerospace treats three to one as the floor.
Noise is everything you didn't ask for, in three families. Raising gain can't buy the ratio back; it lifts signal and noise together.

chips.s: The dominant noise in surface work

beats.narration: Read it on two displays. The impedance plane answers what it is, by phase and shape. The sweep answers where and how many.

tiles.s: Clock position and count per rev

beats.narration: So one rule cuts through the grass. A crack snaps out fast at the notch phase; property and geometry changes drift. Rate, phase, repeatability decide.

payload.sub: Rate of deflection, phase, and repeatability make the call.
